info.title: Transit time calculation V1 and V2
info.description:
Calculates transit time for transport between two locations. Origin and destination can be described using either the id of a defined location or address. If address is used the default location for that post code will be used as origin and/or destination location. There can be multiple transit times for each request. Each transit time is listed together with the PostNord service (with additional services) that the time applies for. For some requests, PostNord does not have detailed transit time, and will instead respond with a day interval (e.g. 3-4 days).

Changes Between V1 (Legacy) and V2

Currently, there are two versions of this endpoint: V1 (Legacy) and V2. Below are the key differences: **Response Handling:** * V1 (Legacy): Transit time requests for a Postnord service that failed to calculate an ETA were filtered out from the response. This often resulted in an empty 200 OK response, causing confusion for consumers, especially when all Postnord service variations failed to calculate an ETA. * V2: Both successful and unsuccessful ETA calculations are included in the response. This provides a more comprehensive view of the ETA calculations. **New Flags:** * V2 introduces two new flags: - isSupported: Indicates if there is at least one depot that can handle either pickup or distribution for this service, regardless of the specific origin/destination.
Logic: * True if any depot exists that supports pickup or distribution for the service. * False if no such depot exists. - isBookable: Indicates if the service can actually be booked for a given origin and destination postal code.
Logic: * Identify pickup depot: Is there a depot that covers pickup for the origin postal code? * Identify distribution depot: Is there a depot that covers distribution for the destination postal code? * Configuration check: If both a pickup and a distribution depot have been found (so normally it should be bookable), but there is a system/administrative error preventing these locations from linking properly, we still conceptually mark isBookable as true (because from a business standpoint, everything needed is in place, but there’s a config error). * If no depots support pickup or distribution for the origin/destination (meaning you cannot fulfill the shipment from start to end), then isBookable is false. These changes aim to enhance transparency and usability by providing more detailed information in the response and reducing confusion caused by empty responses.

Request Changes from V1 to V2

The following changes have been made to the request parameters in version 2 (V2) of the API: * Merged Parameters: - originStreetName and originStreetNumber have been combined into a single parameter: originAddress. This should be formatted as originStreetName + originStreetNumber (e.g., Testvägen 3). - destinationStreetName and destinationStreetNumber have also been combined into a single parameter: destinationAddress. This should be formatted as destinationStreetName + destinationStreetNumber (e.g., Testvägen 3). * Renamed Parameters: - originLocationId is now originProductionPointId. - originLocationType is now originProductionPointType. - originPostCode is now originPostalCode. - destinationLocationId is now destinationProductionPointId. - destinationLocationType is now destinationProductionPointType. - destinationPostCode is now destinationPostalCode. * New Parameters: - A new boolean parameter, showInternalAdditionalServiceCode, has been added. This parameter is false by default. When set to true, it will include the internal additional service code in the response (code).

Response Changes from V1 to V2

The following changes have been made to the response structure in version 2 (V2) of the API: * Grouped Properties: - timeOfDeparture, latestTimeOfBooking, timeOfArrival, eveningPickup, and deliveryPoint are now grouped under the estimatedTimeOfArrival property. * Removed Properties: - The possibleDeviation property has been removed. - The serviceInformation.pickup and serviceInformation.distribution flags have been removed. * Renamed Properties: - serviceInformation is now services. - serviceInformation.serviceCode is now service.basicServiceCode. * New Properties: - additionalServices.additionalServiceCode: This property shows the nordic code equivalent of the local code whenever the showInternalAdditionalServiceCode request flag is set to true. - isSupported: Indicates if there is at least one depot that can handle either pickup or distribution for this service, regardless of the specific origin/destination.
- isBookable: Indicates if the service can actually be booked for a given origin and destination postal code.
- errorMessage: Indicates the cause of the failed ETA calculation Possible errorMessages: - Service X is not offered from/to postal code 'XXXX'. - Could not calculate ETA. - Requested service 'X' not found. - Requested origin/destination 'X' location not found. - Missing foreign transit time for 'Service X' from 'YY' to 'ZZ'.

get.description: Transit times from an address to an address. Response will include all transittimes for relevant country filtered by optional service codes, including possible variations based on additional services.

parameters.name: apikey
parameters.description: The unique consumer (client) identifier 32 characters

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description:
Better described as the hand over time. The time for which PostNord has received the parcel from the customer or 
private sender. Format is local date time

parameters.name: serviceCodes
parameters.description:
Comma-separated PostNord service codes. Only Nordic Codes are allowed. 
For more details, visit PostNord Valid Services & Addon Codes.
* 17 (Mypack Home) 
* 18 (Parcel)
* 19 (Mypack Collect)
* ..

parameters.name: additionalServiceCodes
parameters.description:
Comma-separated PostNord additional service codes. Only Nordic Codes are allowed. 
For more details, visit PostNord Valid Services & Addon Codes.
* D6 (Before 9:00)
* D7 (Before 12:00)
* ..

parameters.name: originStreetName
parameters.description: Street name of origin. Providing this will allow for more accurate results

parameters.name: originStreetNumber
parameters.description: Street number of origin. Providing this will allow for more accurate results

parameters.name: originPostCode
parameters.description: Post code of origin

parameters.name: originCountryCode
parameters.description: Origin country code of address or location as ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

parameters.name: destinationStreetName
parameters.description: Street name of destination. Providing this will allow for more accurate results

parameters.name: destinationStreetNumber
parameters.description: Street number of destination. Providing this will allow for more accurate results

parameters.name: destinationPostCode
parameters.description: Post code of destination

parameters.name: destinationCountryCode
parameters.description: Destination country code of address or location as ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

get.description: Transit times from an address to a location. Response will include all transittimes for relevant country filtered by optional service codes, including possible variations based on additional services. Example usage is to get transit time to a non default service point such as a parcel box.

TransitTimeResponseV2.description:
An array of transit times, with each entry corresponding to a specific service or 
a combination of service and additional service. If the transit time cannot be calculated 
for a service, the entry will include an errorMessage explaining the reason.

isSupported.description:
Indicates if there is at least one depot that can handle either pickup or distribution for this service, regardless of the specific origin/destination. Logic: * True if any depot exists that supports pickup or distribution for the service.
* False if no such depot exists.
Essentially, isSupported is a broad check to determine if the service exists anywhere in the depot network.

isBookable.description:
Indicates if the service can actually be booked for a given origin and destination postal code.

Logic:

* Identify pickup depot: Is there a depot that covers pickup for the origin postal code?
* Identify distribution depot: Is there a depot that covers distribution for the destination postal code?
* Configuration check: If both a pickup and a distribution depot have been found (so normally it should be bookable), but there is a system/administrative error preventing these locations from linking properly, we still conceptually mark isBookable as true (because from a business standpoint, everything needed is in place, but there’s a config error).
* If no depots support pickup or distribution for the origin/destination (meaning you cannot fulfill the shipment from start to end), then isBookable is false.

errorMessage.description:
Only present when Transit Time service was unable to calculate a route
for the given origin, destination, and service. Provides the message explaining the reason for the error.

TransitTimeResponse.description:
Array of transit times. One per service or service and additional service combination. If unable to calculate 
transit time for a service, then that transit time will be filtered from list.

timeOfDeparture.description:
The time that the item is first handled and processed by PostNord. Could for example be the time it is 
handled in a depot if there is pick up at customer or the time it is handled in a service point if it was 
dropped off by a private sender.

latestTimeOfBooking.description:
The latest time for booking for PostNord to be able to pick-up the parcel the same day 
(only applicable for pick-up services). If booking is made before this time, then pick-up same day is possible.
This property is not considered in the transit time calculation

latestTimeForDropOff.description:
The latest time to drop-off at service point for PostNord to pick it up and process the item the same day 
(only applicable for non pick up services, or if origin location was a service point). If item is dropped off 
later than latestTimeForDropOff, then PostNord will attempt to handle item the next day. This property is not 
considered in the transit time calculation

timeOfArrival.description: Estimated time of arrival for the item. Can be null if dayRangeOfArrival is present instead.

possibleDeviation.description: true - if deviation of timeOfArrival can occur depending on exact origin or destination address.

eveningPickUp.description: true - if an evening pickup (after 21:00) was used for this route.

dateOfDeparture.description:
The date that the item is first handled and processed by PostNord.
Could for example be the date it is 

handled in a depot if there is pick up at customer or the date it is
handled in a service point if it was 

dropped off by a private sender.

timeOfDeparture.description:
The date and time that the item is first handled and processed by PostNord.
Could for example be the date and time it is 

handled in a depot if there is pick up at customer or the date and time it 
is handled in a service point if it was 

dropped off by a private sender.

timeOfArrival.description:
Estimated time of arrival for the item. Can be null if
dayRangeOfArrival is present instead.

latestTimeOfBooking.description:
The latest time for booking for PostNord to be able to pick-up the
parcel the same day 

(only applicable for pick-up services). If booking is made before
this time, then pick-up same day is possible.

This property is not considered in the transit time calculation

latestTimeForDropOff.description:
The latest time to drop-off at service point for PostNord to pick it
up and process the item the same day 

(only applicable for non pick up services, or if origin location was
a service point). If item is dropped off 

later than latestTimeForDropOff, then PostNord will attempt to
handle item the next day. This property is not 

considered in the transit time calculation

eveningPickUp.description: true - if an evening pickup (after 21:00) was used for this route.

EstimatedTimeOfArrival.description:
Present when ETA calculation was successful. Contains necessary
information about the planned route, such as Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA).

TransitTimeDayRange.description:
For some country codes or services, an exact time of arrival is not
supported. Instead this property might 

be present to give an estimate of the time of arrival in a day range
format. Can be null if estimatedTimeOfArrival

is present instead.

basicServiceCode.description:
The code of the PostNord product
* 17 (Mypack Home) 
* 18 (Parcel)
* 19 (Mypack Collect)
* ..

name.description:
Name of the PostNord product that the API used to calculated the
transit time

additionalServices.description:
Present if the service code was combined with one or several
additional service codes. Additional service code

can affect the final time of arrival. For example if additional
service code is "before 9"

serviceCode.description:
The code of the PostNord product
* 17 (Mypack Home) 
* 18 (Parcel)
* 19 (Mypack Collect)
* ..

groupCode.description:
Think of it as country code or "DPD". Valid values are;
 * SE for issuerCode=Z12
 * DK for issuerCode=Z11
 * NO for issuerCode=Z13
 * FI for issuerCode=Z14
 * DPD for DPD services

name.description: Name of the PostNord product that the API used to calculated the transit time

pickup.description:
True - if service supports pickup at customer by default.
False - if service instead supports drop off at service point by default

distribution.description:
True - if service supports distribution by default
False - if service instead supports collection at a service point by default

additionalServices.description:
Present if the service code was combined with one or several additional service codes. Additional service code
can affect the final time of arrival. For example if additional service code is "before 9"

locationId.description: The production point ID of the service point

countryCode.description: Country code  of the service point as ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

name.description: The name of the service point

address.description: Street name and number of the delivery point

isExpressDeliveryPoint.description:
True - if the service was express parcel. Not present if it wasn't
an express parcel

collectionTime.description:
Shows the time of collection for express parcel. Only present if
isExpressDeliveryPoint = true

TransitTimeDeliveryPointDto.description:
Present if the last link is a collection at a service point or if the
service was express service parcel (42). 

If the last link was collection at a service point, then this object
will describe which service point that was 

chosen as part of the planned route (either default service point or the
one in the destination parameter). If 

service code is express parcel, then this will show which service point
that handles the express parcel and 

the collection time as well.

additionalServiceCode.description:
The nordic code equivalent of the additional service
* D6 (Before 9:00)
* D7 (Before 12:00)
* ..

OkTransitTimeResponse.description:
OK. Was able to attempt to calculate transit times for given request. However, it is not guaranteed that there 
will be any transit times in response. Response might be empty array. Reasons for empty array might be many, such
as:
* No first or last location was found for given post codes
* PostNord does not support routing for given from and to and the service code
* The route has yet to be configured in the transport information system

OkTransitTimeResponseV2.description:
OK. Indicates whether the transit times could be calculated for the given request. 
The response may include either an 'estimatedTimeOfArrival' for successful calculations or an 
errorMessage for unsuccessful ones.
Possible reasons for a response with an errorMessage include:

* No first or last location was found for given post codes

* PostNord does not support routing for given from and to and the
service code

* The route has yet to be configured in the transport information system

value.errorMessage: Service SE-22 is not offered from postal code 17173
Service SE-22 is not offered from postal code 17173

BadRequest.description:
The server cannot or will not process the request due to something that
is perceived to be a client error (e.g., malformed request syntax,
invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing)

Fault codes:
* Missing required field API-008
* Invalid input API-009

Unauthenticated.description: API-key or authentication token is missing/invalid.

MethodNotAccepted.description:
The method received in the request-line is known by the origin server
but not supported by the target resource.

InternalServerError.description:
The server is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary
overload or scheduled maintenance, which will likely be alleviated after
some delay.

TemporarilyUnavailable.description:
The server is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary
overload or scheduled maintenance, which will likely be alleviated after
some delay.
